model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Purdue is a dominant 2-seed ranked 8th nationally with the #1 adjusted offense in the country (AdjO 131-132.6) and a +30.5 adjusted efficiency margin, while Queens is a 15-seed ranked 181st in KenPom with a negative efficiency margin (-1.45) and the 316th-ranked defense nationally. The roughly 32-point efficiency gap per 100 possessions is staggering, and Queens' historically poor defense will be completely overwhelmed by Purdue's elite Big Ten-tested scoring attack. This matchup is even more lopsided than the typical 2/15 game, making Purdue one of the safest picks in the entire bracket.

reasoningStep.title: West 2/15: Purdue vs Queens
reasoningStep.summary: Purdue's #1 national offense and 32-point efficiency margin advantage over Queens make this one of the most lopsided matchups in the tournament. Queens' 316th-ranked defense has no realistic path to containing Purdue's elite scoring.
evidence: Purdue: KenPom #8, AdjO 131.0 (#2), AdjD 100.5 (#36), AdjEM +30.5, record 26-8 (Big Ten)
Queens: KenPom #181, AdjO 115.8 (#78), AdjD 117.3 (#316), AdjEM -1.45, record 21-13 (ASUN)
Efficiency gap of ~32 points per 100 possessions is among the largest in the tournament
Historical 2-seed vs 15-seed win rate ~94%, and this specific matchup is more lopsided than the typical 2/15 game
Queens' 316th-ranked defense faces the nation's #1 offense — a catastrophic stylistic mismatch

modelTrace.content:
The search tools are having issues, but the ratings data provides a very clear picture. Let me finalize my analysis.

**Key findings:**

- **Purdue**: 2-seed, ranked **8th** in both KenPom and Torvik. Record: 26-8 (Big Ten). Elite offense ranked **#1-2 nationally** (AdjO 131-132.6). Solid defense (AdjD 100.2-100.5, ~36th). Adjusted efficiency margin: **+30.5**.
- **Queens**: 15-seed, ranked **181st KenPom / 199th Torvik**. Record: 21-13 (ASUN). Decent offense (AdjO 114.6-115.8, ~78-85th) but terrible defense (AdjD 117.1-117.3, **316th nationally**). Adjusted efficiency margin: **-1.45**.

The efficiency gap is approximately **32 points per 100 possessions** — one of the largest in the entire tournament. Queens' 316th-ranked defense will face the **#1 offense in the country**. Historically, 2-seeds beat 15-seeds about 94% of the time, and this matchup is even more lopsided than the typical 2/15 game.
